questions.title: In Episode 4 Ivar makes a big achievement on Mandor?

red.answer: He was promoted to a Sarth Lord

green.answer: He infiltrated the One True Leader of Gorum's Inner Circle

questions.title: Line 577 of bat_v_elp.c in Linux kernel
questions.explanation: B.A.T.M.A.N. is a routing protocol for multi-hop mobile ad hoc networks which is under development by the German Freifunk community.

questions.title: What is the airspeed velocity of an unladen swallow?

green.answer: What do you mean? An African or European swallow?

red.answer: See you on the other side
Some already tried, but you cannot ask them anymore

green.answer: We'll never know, but it's probably even good for you

questions.title: Array indexes: do they start at 0 or 1?
questions.explanation: 0-based indexing is used by C, Java, Python, JavaScript and most modern languages. 1-based indexing is used by MATLAB, Lua, and R. Both conventions exist — but 0 is correct in any serious language.

red.answer: 1 — because the first element IS element one

green.answer: 0 — because memory offsets start at zero

questions.title: Tabs or spaces for indentation?
questions.explanation: Python PEP 8 recommends spaces. The Linux kernel uses tabs. The debate has been raging since 1978 and no one has won.

red.answer: Tabs — one character, configurable width, semantically correct

both.answer: It doesn't matter as long as you're consistent

questions.title: Half-Life 3: confirmed or not?
questions.explanation: As of 2024, Half-Life 3 has not been officially announced. Half-Life: Alyx (2020) is the closest thing. The silence is deafening.

questions.title: TCP vs UDP: which one guarantees delivery?
questions.explanation: TCP (Transmission Control Protocol) guarantees ordered, reliable delivery with acknowledgements. UDP is fire-and-forget — faster but no guarantees.

questions.title: You use a regular expression to solve a problem. Now you have:
questions.explanation: This is a famous quote by Jamie Zawinski: "Some people, when confronted with a problem, think 'I know, I'll use regular expressions.' Now they have two problems."

questions.title: Linus Torvalds created Linux. What else did he create?
questions.explanation: Linus Torvalds created Git in 2005 in just 10 days, after the Linux kernel community lost access to BitKeeper. He named it after himself — 'git' is British slang for an unpleasant person.

questions.title: Dark mode is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is scientifically obvious to any developer who has accidentally opened a white terminal at 2am.

green.answer: The only correct way to use a computer

questions.title: In IEEE 754 floating point: is 0.1 + 0.2 == 0.3?
questions.explanation: 0.1 + 0.2 in IEEE 754 double precision is 0.30000000000000004, not 0.3. This trips up every developer at least once. Never use floating point for money.

green.answer: No — floating point rounding makes it 0.30000000000000004

questions.title: In The Matrix, Neo is offered two pills. Which does he take?
questions.explanation: Neo takes the red pill to learn the truth about the Matrix. The blue pill would have let him remain in blissful ignorance. This scene is so iconic it has become a cultural meme.

questions.title: How do you exit Vim?
questions.explanation: :q! force-quits without saving. This is one of the most searched questions on Stack Overflow — a question about exiting Vim has over 2 million views.

questions.title: Is a hot dog a sandwich?

green.answer: Mister Green, I don't want to do this anymore

questions.title: What's on The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxies cover.
